data.title: Uber Eats starts deliveries from Dollar General
data.description:
• Uber Eats (NYSE:UBER) will begin making deliveries from 14,000 Dollar General (DG) and pOpshelf stores, with orders now available on the Uber Eats platform.
  • To celebrate the launch, Uber Eats (NYSE:UBER) is offering 40% off Dollar General (DG) and pOpshelf orders of $20 or more. Uber One (NYSE:UBER) members qualify for free delivery on eligible items.
  • The latest collaboration with Dollar General (DG) adds to Uber’s (NYSE:UBER) current stable of partnerships including Costco (COST), Starbucks (SBUX), Instacart (CART), and Domino’s (DPZ).
  • “Our goal is to make it effortless for people to go anywhere and get anything,” said Dara Khosrowshahi, Uber’s CEO.
  • Shares of Uber (UBER) and Dollar General (DG) both opened higher on Friday.

data.title: VivoPower to purchase initial $100 million of Ripple shares
data.description:
VivoPower International (NASDAQ:VVPR) Friday said that it would acquire Ripple Labs shares as part of a strategic enhancement to its XRP-focused digital asset treasury strategy, sending shares up 10% in morning trade.

Following two months of due diligence, VivoPower has budgeted to purchase an initial $100 million of privately held Ripple shares.

Aside from these transactions, VivoPower (NASDAQ:VVPR) will continue to directly acquire and hold XRP tokens.

VivoPower will become the first and only publicly listed company in the United States that provides shareholders with exposure to Ripple shares as well as XRP tokens, the company said.

For every $10 million of Ripple shares acquired, an estimated $5.15 per share of value accumulation could accrue to VivoPower (VVPR) shareholders.

Backed by investors including Andreessen Horowitz and Google Ventures, Ripple Labs (Ripple) holds 41 billion XRP tokens, representing about 41% of all XRP tokens on issue.

data.title: TeraWulf stock rises after Q2 loss slightly better than feared amid HPC expansion
data.description:
TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) on Friday posted a second-quarter net loss came in slightly better than feared as the company continued to develop sustainable digital infrastructure to expand its high-performance computing ("HPC") hosting and bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining businesses. Shares gained 3% minutes after the opening bell.

Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.05, vs. the -$0.06 average analyst estimate, improved from -$0.16 in the prior quarter and widened from -$0.03 in Q2 2024.

Revenue of $47.6M, trailing the $49.1M consensus, advanced from $34.4M in the prior quarter and $35.6M a year earlier.

“We continue to see strong demand from enterprise and hyperscale customers for low-cost, zero-carbon compute infrastructure," said CEO Paul Prager.

Starting in Q3, CFO Patrick Fleury noted, TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) will start recognizing revenue from HPC hosting, "marking a key inflection point in our financial profile."

During the three-month period, the company self-mined 485 bitcoin (BTC-USD) at the Lake Mariner Facility, up from 372 in Q1 and down from 699 bitcoin in Q2 2024. The Y/Y decline was mainly driven by the April 2024 halving and last year's divestiture of the Nautilus Cryptomine facility.

Prager noted WULF is in advanced talks to "expand HPC hosting deployments at Lake Mariner and are actively pursuing additional sites to support our long-term growth pipeline.”

Adjusted EBITDA jumped to $14.5M from -$4.7M in Q1, but fell from $19.5M a year ago.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow inch up as tech stocks show strength
data.description:
Wall Street ticked up on Friday, amid a rally in tech stocks that boosted market sentiment, even as investors continued to weigh the impact of Donald Trump's tariffs.

The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.4%, the Dow (DJI) +0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.4%.  

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.27%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.76%.

Wall Street's major averages on Thursday closed mixed, as positive sentiment sparked by U.S. President Donald Trump's chip tariff exemptions and progress on Russia-Ukraine de-escalation petered out.

"Markets have been mixed over the past 24 hours, with the S&P 500 edging -0.08% lower, partly weighed down by disappointing earnings from Eli Lilly, even as the Nasdaq (+0.35%) and the Mag-7 (+0.40%) reached new all-time highs," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Trump's reciprocal tariffs took effect at midnight on Thursday, and traders will keep a watch on the impact the trade policies have going ahead.

"On the trade front, Trump’s tariffs officially came into effect yesterday, though markets showed little reaction," Reid said.

In other news, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller is reportedly gaining momentum in the process to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the central bank.

There is not much economic data on Friday. St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem will speak in an event hosted by Mississippi Valley State University.

data.title: Akamai's Q2 results get largely neutral reactions at Piper Sandler
data.description:
Shares of Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) rose about 1% premarket on Friday after second quarter results beat estimates and the company raised its full year outlook, drawing largely neutral views at Piper Sandler.

Piper Sandler kept its Neutral rating on Akamai but lowered the price target on the shares to $83 from $88.

"Following a reset of shares over the last few weeks, shares are pointed slightly higher afterhours as Akamai was able to beat and raise for the year, though some of these gains were given back at the realization that the raise was primarily due to FX, TikTok inclusion, and CDN [Content Delivery Network] upside that offset Compute missing and Security coming inline," said analysts led by James Fish.

The analysts added that the company has many moving parts to it, even within sub-segments, and it will take some time to work through this transition. If successful, the transition could result in growth acceleration over the next few years back into at least over mid-single-digit territory, but if not, there is opportunity to take out costs to drive better profitability and free cash flow, or FCF.

"We see shares as range-bound still and reiterate our Neutral rating, with PT [price target] moving to $83 on lower FCF estimates," said Fish and his team.

Related stock: Cloudflare (NET) largely flat premarket on Friday.

data.title: LM Funding America’s bitcoin treasury valued $17.8M at July-end
data.description:
• LM Funding America (NASDAQ:LMFA) mined 5.9 bitcoins last month, bringing its bitcoin holdings on July 31, 2025 to 150.4.
  • The company estimates that the value of its 150.4 bitcoin holdings was around $17.8 million or $3.461 per share, based on a bitcoin price of ~$118,100 as of July 31, 2025.
  • Bruce Rodgers, chairman and CEO of LM Funding, commented “In July, we reallocated a portion of our Bitcoin treasury to fund capacity expansion. July’s 7% increase in production and 20% growth in energy revenue reflect the reinvestment of treasury assets into operations—advancing our long-term Bitcoin accumulation strategy.”
  • LMFA +6.8% premarket.
